Four people are arrested on suspicion of murder after death of young woman who was found unconscious in the street

Four people have been arrested on suspicion of murder in Leeds after an unidentified young woman who was found unconscious in the street has died. Officers rushed to Kennerleigh Avenue in Austhorpe at 5.55am today following reports that a woman, believed to be a teenager, was lying unconscious in the street. She was found to have suffered serious injuries and was taken to the hospital for immediate medical treatment, but was pronounced dead shortly afterwards. Police have been unable to identify the woman and have released details of her description in the hope that someone might recognise her, so her next of kin can be notified.

My beautiful daughter went to our GP with a very sore back and tingling in her legs, for almost a year she was told it was sciatica. The hospital lost her scans and notes, she was eventually diagnosed with a sarcoma tumor in her spine and died 15 weeks later. We, as a family are beyond devastated, our life is horrific without her. I wish doctors would take people seriously and not just fob them off with pain meds or antibiotics.

A dozen Hertfordshire officers arrived at the home of IT boss Sam Smith after the force received complaints from two Facebook group members. Mr Smith's house was searched on March 8 last year, his devices were seized and he spent a night in a cell at Hatfield police station. Detectives dropped the case six days later, noting in the police log that the search was not 'suitable or lawful.' Mr Smith, 47, is now suing the force through the High Court for £70,000 for damages and personal injury, claiming the stress of the arrest had caused a recurrence of an eye condition.

  • 1 video
  • 38 shares Wrongful WhatsApp arrest police threw man in a cell over blog post

Is James Bond becoming a joke? Spy film series shaken and stirred by new show that takes aim at flailing franchise amid fears over Amazon's new input and a never-ending stream of 007 frontrunners

For more than seven decades, James Bond has been deemed one of the most iconic characters in film, with his trademark look as recognisable as the martinis he sips. But since Daniel Craig departed the role in 2021, it seems that the franchise's future has become more comical than ever, leaving die-hard fans wondering whether it'll ever manage to get back on track. Indeed, it seems that at one point or another any major actor has been in the running to play the new spy, with Jessie Buckley, who has never been linked with the franchise, even tipped to be the next Bond Girl after her Oscar win earlier this month.

This is the moment a police officer was stabbed in the neck in an unprovoked attack by a knife-wielding yob, risking his life to protect his colleagues. Sergeant Tim Ansell, who this week received a bravery award from King Charles, threw himself between the attacker and three officers in Whalley Range, Manchester in July 2023. The group had been responding to a road traffic accident when, Jacob Brown, 27, who was not involved in the crash, approached them from behind with a knife.
